description: Photorealistic miniature/tiny people in real scenes

model._hint: Transformer: city96/FLUX.1-schnell-gguf (flux1-schnell-Q8_0.gguf, ~12 GB). Pipeline components: black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell (gated — HF token required). LoRA: iliketoasters/miniature-people (Miniature People - v1-0.safetensors). Q8_0 keeps near-bfloat16 quality with trivial dequantisation overhead on MPS. sequential_cpu_offload disabled (incompatible with GGUF). LoRA applied via set_adapters() (unfused — required for GGUF). | gguf_file is the transformer only. base_model_id supplies VAE, text encoders, scheduler.

lora._hint: 0.9 default. Range: 0.7–1.0. Lower values blend with base FLUX aesthetics.

generation._hint: 4 steps (schnell sweet spot). Range: 1–8. More steps do not meaningfully improve quality. | FLUX.1-schnell is CFG-distilled → guidance_scale MUST be 0.0. Any positive value degrades quality. | FLUX native 1024×1024. Landscape (1024×768) works well for wide scene shots.

system._hint: ~12 GB peak; sequential_cpu_offload: disabled (incompatible with GGUF)

notes.about: Generates photorealistic images of miniature-scale people placed in real-world scenes — tiny figures next to everyday objects like keyboards, coins, or cups. Solves the 'plastic figure' problem typical of AI miniature images. V1 — further iterations planned.
notes.prompt_guide: TRIGGER WORDS: use one per prompt — 'miniature man', 'miniature woman', 'miniature people'. Describe scenes that imply scale using real-world reference objects (keyboard, coin, truck tire, matchbox). Closeups do not work well in v1. If clothing is not described, plaid patterns may appear. Examples: 'miniature man standing next to a coffee cup, photorealistic', 'miniature woman on top of a coin, macro photography', 'miniature people on a keyboard key, studio lighting'.
